forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,30 @@
+---
+depends_on:
+- 01KXHBDW50GFJS4TH0HGS0D3KP
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '9280'
+title: 'ACP→Transcript utility: SessionUpdate stream into a FoundationModels Transcript'
+---
+## What
+Ship the symmetric half of the bridge in this package (spec §9, §10 — decided 2026-07-14: ship here so AgentViewKit consumes any ACP agent uniformly), in `Sources/FoundationModelsACP/Bridge/`:
+
+- A client-side utility that folds an `AsyncStream<SessionUpdate>` into a growing FoundationModels `Transcript`:
+  - `agent_message_chunk` → `.response` text
+  - `tool_call` / `tool_call_update` → `.toolCalls` / `.toolOutput` (correlated by `toolCallId`)
+  - `agent_thought_chunk`, `plan`, and permission requests → custom `.structure` segments
+- The ACP client thereby becomes just another producer of the same Transcript AgentViewKit already renders — the inverse of the §7 mapping.
+- Round-trip property: a turn mapped FM → ACP (bridge task) and back through this utility reproduces an equivalent Transcript (lossless round-trip, spec §7).
+- Apply the same straggler policy as the session-update stream task: late `tool_call_update`s still fold in.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Each SessionUpdate variant folds into the documented Transcript entry type
+- [ ] Round-trip test: scripted Transcript → bridge → SessionUpdate stream → this utility → Transcript, equivalent modulo documented lossy fields (none expected)
+- [ ] Late tool_call_update after the turn's final message still updates the corresponding toolOutput
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `Tests/FoundationModelsACPTests/Bridge/ACPToTranscriptTests.swift` — per-variant folding + straggler handling
+- [ ] `Tests/FoundationModelsACPTests/Bridge/RoundTripTests.swift` — FM → ACP → FM equivalence on golden turns
+- [ ] Run `swift test` — exits 0
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,4 +1,27 @@
 ---
+comments:
+- actor: wballard
+  id: 01kxksd9kfvqsr2zfsxseh0s8c
+  text: |-
+    Picked up. Implemented the ACP→Transcript utility as the exact inverse of the prompt-turn task's TranscriptMapper (FM→ACP).
+
+    FM Transcript IS client-constructible (verified against the SDK via the existing BridgeTestSupport helpers + a clean `swift build`): `Transcript(entries:)`, `Transcript.Response(assetIDs:segments:)`, `Transcript.Reasoning(segments:)`, `Transcript.ToolCalls([Transcript.ToolCall(id:toolName:arguments:)])`, `Transcript.ToolOutput(id:toolName:segments:)`, `Transcript.TextSegment(content:)`, `Transcript.StructuredSegment(schemaName:content:)`, `GeneratedContent(json:)`/`.jsonString`. `Transcript` and `Transcript.ToolCalls` are Collections (Array(transcript) / Array(calls) compile). No invented initializers.
+
+    NEW Sources/.../Bridge/TranscriptBuilder.swift — public, stateful struct (mirrors TranscriptMapper's shape):
+    - `public mutating func fold(_ SessionUpdate)` + `public var transcript: Transcript` (grows) + `public static func transcript(folding: AsyncStream<SessionUpdate>) async -> Transcript` (drains a stream — the AgentViewKit entry point).
+    - Inverse mapping: agent_message_chunk → text segment(s) accumulated into one `.response`; agent_thought_chunk → text segment(s) into one `.reasoning` (FIRST-CLASS reasoning entry, §10); a role change or any non-chunk update flushes the open group. tool_call → `.toolCalls([ToolCall(id=toolCallId, toolName=title, arguments=GeneratedContent(json: rawInput))])`. tool_call_update → `.toolOutput(id=toolCallId, segments from text content)`. plan → `.response` with a `.structure` segment (schemaName "plan", content = encoded Plan) so the forward mapper re-derives it. user_message_chunk/mode/config/usage/sessionInfo/availableCommands/unknown → skipped (no agent-output entry).
+    - TOOL-NAME RECOVERY (correlation by id): ToolCallUpdate carries no toolName, but ToolOutput requires one. The builder remembers toolName per toolCallId from the preceding tool_call and applies it to the correlated tool_call_update → LOSSLESS on toolName (no orphan default needed for well-formed turns; orphan straggler defaults to "").
+
+    LOSSLESSNESS / documented lossy fields: the ACP update stream is a projection; fields the projection drops (Response.assetIDs, Reasoning.signature/metadata, non-text content blocks, ToolOutput.toolName except via id-correlation) are reconstructed empty. On turns produced by TranscriptMapper those are already empty and toolName is recovered → round trip is lossless (matches the card's "none expected"). Documented in the type doc.
+
+    ROUND-TRIP PROPERTY: equivalence = identical forward projection (the SessionUpdate sequence is exactly what AgentViewKit renders). RoundTripTests: scripted Transcript → TranscriptMapper (the bridge) → [SessionUpdate] → AsyncStream → TranscriptBuilder → Transcript' → TranscriptMapper → assert reprojection == original projection. Golden turns: full text+reasoning+2-tool-call turn, reasoning+response, plan, and a straggler turn (tool output settling AFTER the final message) — all lossless. Plus a structural test proving toolName is recovered through the round trip.
+
+    STRAGGLER: covered both ways — ACPToTranscriptTests (bare late tool_call_update after final message folds into a tool output) and RoundTripTests (scripted late-settling output).
+
+    Tests: NEW Tests/.../Bridge/ACPToTranscriptTests.swift (10: per-variant folding, accumulation, role-change flush, id correlation, straggler, plan, skip, stream drain) + Tests/.../Bridge/RoundTripTests.swift (5). Deterministic, no live model, reuses the prompt-turn TranscriptMapper + scripted entry helpers. swift build --build-tests 0 warnings/0 errors; swift test = 171 FoundationModelsACPTests (+15) + 108 ACPGenerateTests = 279 pass, 0 failures.
+
+    Note for downstream (^e2x6ra0 e2e, ^0td21b4 README): the shipped client-side utility is `TranscriptBuilder` — fold a SessionUpdate stream (e.g. ClientSideConnection.updates(for:)) into a growing FoundationModels Transcript; it is the §7/§9 inverse so an ACP client becomes another producer of the Transcript AgentViewKit renders.
+  timestamp: 2026-07-15T21:02:34.095675+00:00
 depends_on:
 - 01KXHBDW50GFJS4TH0HGS0D3KP
 position_column: doing
